# BI-260023: Implement Enter-to-Search with Multi-Label Chip Input

**Status**: Ready
**Type**: Feature Implementation
**Module**: workspace-search
**Component**: SearchBar.tsx, use-search.ts
**Blocks**: Search MVP completion

## Dependencies

- REQ-FR-260024: Search Execution on Enter Key
- REQ-FR-260025: Multi-Label Chip Input with Space/Colon Delimiters
- REQ-FR-260023: Recommended Labels Visibility
- REQ-FR-260022: Blank Initial Workspace
- ADR-260023: Retire Search Bootstrap (C1 Only)

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Chip creation works**: Pressing Space or Colon after typing a label name creates a visual chip inside the input field
2. **Input clears after chip**: After chip creation, text input is empty and ready for the next label
3. **Multiple chips stack**: User can add multiple label chips in sequence
4. **Backspace removes last chip**: Pressing Backspace when input is empty removes the trailing chip
5. **Enter submits search**: Pressing Enter executes search with all chips (labels) included
6. **No keystroke filtering**: Typing does NOT trigger live filtering; search only executes on Enter
7. **Recommended labels still show**: Three random labels from list_labels remain visible as optional filter chips (from BI-260021)

## Implementation Slice

### Phase 1: Chip State Management
- Add chip state to SearchBar.tsx or use-search.ts
- Track array of selected label chips
- Separate "input text" from "committed chips"

### Phase 2: Key Handlers
- Intercept Space and Colon key presses
- On Space/Colon: convert input text to chip, clear input, add chip to array
- On Backspace (when input empty): remove last chip from array
- On Enter: submit search with all chips and any remaining text

### Phase 3: Chip Rendering
- Render chips inside search input (before text input cursor)
- Add visual styling (balloon/chip appearance)
- Include removal affordance (optional X button, or rely on Backspace)

### Phase 4: Search Integration
- Update search query to include all committed chips as label filters
- Execute search only on Enter (not on every keystroke)
- Ensure use-search.ts does not live-filter on input changes

## Testing

- Unit tests in SearchBar.test.tsx and use-search.test.ts
- E2E test in search-workspace.spec.ts (verify chip creation, multi-label search, Enter submission)
- Manual verification: type label → space → chip appears → input clears → repeat → enter submits

## Notes

- This work supersedes previous live-filtering behavior (addresses item 8 in instruction-update-todo)
- Related to item 7 (search data freshness): search query should hit backend on Enter, not use client-cached filtering
- Defers autocomplete (item 9) for future work
